page: Google Cloud Platform t2d-standard-60 is a general-purpose server instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor based on the x86_64 architecture. It features 60 dedicated physical cores and 60 vCPUs, operating with a 1.0 thread-per-core ratio. The instance is configured with 240.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or integrated GPUs.

In benchmark evaluations, the t2d-standard-60 demonstrates top-tier multi-core CPU performance, scoring 141,982.77 in stress-ng multi-core tests and achieving 520.43 GB/sec in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression. Cached memory read bandwidth is exceptionally high at 6,846.14 GB/sec, though uncached large-block read and write speeds fall into the average tier at 141.75 GB/sec and 64.61 GB/sec respectively. Single-core performance is strong, as evidenced by a stress-ng single-core score of 2,693.79 and a Geekbench single-core score of 1,601.0.

This hardware profile is highly suited for multi-threaded computational tasks, software compilation, and database operations, supported by a Passmark database score of 26,072.24 and 86.31 million operations per second in Redis SET benchmarks. While prompt processing for small and medium language models is highly efficient, the lack of a dedicated GPU results in weak performance for large-scale LLM text generation, making it less suitable for heavy AI inference workloads.
description: Google Cloud Platform t2d-standard-60 is a general-purpose server instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7B13 processor with 60 dedicated physical cores and 60 vCPUs on the x86_64 architecture. Equipped with 240.0 GB of RAM (4.0 GB per core) and no local storage or GPUs, it offers a balanced resource profile. Benchmarks reveal top-tier multi-core CPU performance and cached memory bandwidth, alongside strong capabilities in software compilation, database operations, and multi-threaded decompression. However, uncached memory bandwidth and single-core operations remain average, and the absence of GPU acceleration limits its utility for large-scale language model text generation. This instance is best suited for multi-threaded enterprise workloads, Redis databases, and software development pipelines.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform t2d-standard-60 is a general-purpose server featuring 60 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 240 GB RAM, and top-tier multi-core performance for databases and multi-threaded workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform t2d-standard-60 features 60 AMD EPYC vCPUs and 240 GB RAM, delivering strong multi-core performance for enterprise workloads.
tagline: A dedicated 60-core AMD EPYC instance delivering balanced memory and top-tier multi-threaded performance for enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by 60 dedicated AMD EPYC 7B13 physical cores on x86_64 architecture
Equipped with 240 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core
Top-tier multi-core CPU performance and multi-threaded decompression capabilities
Strong performance in database operations, software compilation, and Redis caching
No local storage or GPU acceleration, limiting large-scale AI text generation
